title: BugResolved payload
description: Emitted when a bug closes via bug_resolve. Kind-discriminated: the `kind` field names the resolution. The five kinds match the existing bug.status enum (fixed/wontfix/upstream/dup/routed). Field-shape requirements per kind live in the bug_resolve handler (e.g. routed→fixed requires commit_sha); the schema enforces only the kind enum + nullable typing so the existing handler reality validates without retrofit. For kind=routed, the routed task is also recorded in envelope refs.related_entities so projection folds can join without re-parsing the payload.

kind.description: Resolution kind. `upstream` (bug 1330) means real, reproducible, traceable to a dependency we don't author and therefore not fixed locally — distinct from `wontfix` (out of scope / WAI). Verb-form aliases (fix/route/duplicate/external/externalized/upstreamed/wont_fix) normalise to these enum values inside the handler before the event is emitted.

commit_sha.description: For kind=fixed: the commit that landed the fix. Accepts the literal sentinel 'unversioned' for fixes whose artifacts live outside any git repo (user-level scripts, vault docs) per mcp__toolkit-server__work convention. May be null when the fix lands separately and gets stamped later via bug_stamp_sha (which emits its own BugStamped event).

dup_of.description: For kind=dup: the slug of the bug this one duplicates. The current bug_resolve handler does not yet have a typed dup_of param — dup pointers historically live in resolution_note; this field stays optional so the event validates either way.

routed_chain_slug.description: For kind=routed: the chain that absorbed this bug's work. null for other kinds.

routed_task_slug.description: For kind=routed: the specific task in routed_chain_slug that executes the fix. null for other kinds.

routed_suggestion_slug.description: T5-bugs additive bump (2026-05-21): for kind=routed routing to a suggestion, the suggestion's slug. null for other kinds. The bug_resolve reroute path SETs the column in CRUD but pre-T5 the BugResolved emit didn't include it; payload-only fold couldn't reconstruct.

resolution_note.description: Free-text note from the resolver. For kind=fixed: what changed beyond what the commit shows. For kind=wontfix / upstream: reasoning. For kind=dup: free text supplementing dup_of (currently the only place the dup target is recorded — see dup_of note). For kind=routed: may be null since the routed_* pointers carry the load. Distinct from envelope.rationale (required for agent actors, answers 'why this resolution'); resolution_note is operational context for downstream readers.
